_notes: 1. Word Organization: Extract 15 core words per unit. Split into Group A, B, and C (5 words each). Maintain a balanced distribution of parts of speech. The 'pos' MUST be determined by the word's specific usage in the original test questions.

2. Passage Writing (Levels A2-C1): Provide four distinct difficulty levels for each group. Ensure tone and vocabulary are appropriate for each CEF level (A2: Basic, B1: Intermediate, B2: Upper-Inter, C1: Advanced).
3. Master Challenge (C2 Level): Every unit must include a 'GroupBonus' story that integrates all 15 words from that unit into a coherent narrative.
4. Text Markup: Highlight target words strictly using the **word** syntax. Do not use other markdown styles (like italics or underlining) within stories to ensure smooth parsing.

5. Dictionary Entries: Each word must contain 'pos' (part of speech), 'translation' (Traditional Chinese), 'definition' (English), and 'example' + 'example_tw'. The 'example' sentence MUST be adopted directly from the original test questions.

6. JSON Layout & Optimization: Use 'Compact Mode'. Every word object in 'wordDetails' and every story entry in 'stories' should be on a single line. This minimizes file height, improves scrolling speed, and supports massive future expansion.

7. Answer Sequence Variation: The appearance order of the 5 target words MUST be completely shuffled and different across the four levels (A2-C1) within the same group to prevent pattern memorization.
8. Language Localization: All Chinese content must be presented strictly in Traditional Chinese (Taiwan).

9. Case Sensitivity & Uniformity: To ensure flawless interaction with the web application, all target words in the 'words' arrays and all keys in the 'wordDetails' objects MUST be strictly lowercase. The HTML app uses case-insensitive matching (.toLowerCase()) internally, so **word** tags within stories may use sentence-case capitalization if needed, but base arrays and dictionary keys must remain lowercase to prevent hint giveaways and lookup errors.

high_achiever.name: 高分好手
high_achiever.description: 在任何測驗中獲得90分以上。

effortful_learner.name: 努力不懈
effortful_learner.description: 在任何測驗中獲得60分以上。

a2_explorer.description: 完成一個單元內所有組別的一顆星難度測驗（80分以上）。

cute_cat.name: 可愛貓貓
cute_cat.description: 是的，你沒看錯，宇宙的盡頭是貓貓。恭喜你完成所有挑戰，這隻可愛的橘貓是你的了。

stories.text: The hotel room has a nice **view** of the sea. Before our **departure** tomorrow, we need to pack our bags. I will **attach** a red name tag to my suitcase. Traveling to new places helps to **expand** my mind. I have **derived** a lot of joy from this wonderful trip.

To **expand** your small business, you need to advertise more online. Please **attach** your resume to the email before sending it. The train's early **departure** surprised all the waiting passengers. He **derived** great satisfaction from helping the poor. From his point of **view**, the charity plan is absolutely perfect.

She **derived** deep inspiration from her travels across Europe last summer. In her **view**, experiencing different cultures is essential for personal growth. We plan to **expand** the tour route to include more historic cities next year. Please make sure to **attach** the detailed itinerary to your final report before your **departure**.

The sudden and unannounced **departure** of the CEO left the entire company in shock. The new managing director **derived** her innovative strategy from years of industry experience. In her professional **view**, the only logical way forward is to aggressively **expand** into overseas markets. She instructed the team to **attach** the revised financial projections to the final proposal.

We finally arrived at our holiday **destination** near the beach. Eating fresh fruit is very **beneficial** for your body. I had to **cancel** my guitar lesson because I was sick today. The strict teacher **restricts** the use of cell phones in her class. Next week, I will **undergo** a small eye test at the hospital.

Due to the terrible snowstorm, we had to **cancel** the evening flight. Our final **destination** was a beautiful small island in the south. The old wooden bridge must **undergo** major repairs soon for safety. Regular daily exercise is highly **beneficial** to the human body. The doctor carefully **restricts** his patients from eating too much sugar.

The professional athlete had to **undergo** knee surgery after the severe injury. The demanding coach strictly **restricts** the entire team's diet before a crucial match. This specific kind of physical therapy is incredibly **beneficial** for a fast recovery. Unfortunately, they had to **cancel** their planned trip to the summer training camp. However, their ultimate **destination** remains winning the world championship.

The newly implemented environmental law severely **restricts** industrial carbon emissions in the urban area. Consequently, the manufacturing company was forced to **cancel** its massive expansion plans and **undergo** a comprehensive ecological audit. Reaching a truly sustainable **destination** in modern corporate practices is now their primary goal. Experts agree that these drastic changes will ultimately prove **beneficial** to the entire global ecosystem.

I really love the beautiful **scenes** of the forest in this movie. During the heavy rainstorm, we had a long power **blackout**. The English teacher **introduced** a new student to our class today. The clear view from the top of the mountain was absolutely **spectacular**. Running fast in the school hallways is strictly **prohibited**.

There was a sudden and scary power **blackout** in the city last night. However, without the city lights, the stars looked **spectacular** in the dark sky. Smoking is absolutely **prohibited** inside any public building. The popular nature documentary showed amazing **scenes** of wild animals hunting. The friendly tour guide **introduced** us to many kinds of local birds.

The creative head chef **introduced** a unique spicy flavor to the traditional dish. Using any artificial food colors is strictly **prohibited** in this famous restaurant. The busy kitchen was chaotic, with frantic cooking **scenes** everywhere you looked. A sudden electrical **blackout** forced the staff to use emergency candles. Despite this major challenge, the meal they served was truly **spectacular**.

The national museum's new historical exhibition is nothing short of **spectacular**. The passionate curator enthusiastically **introduced** several rare, ancient artifacts to the visiting public. During a brief but unexpected electrical **blackout**, the advanced security system fortunately remained active. Flash photography is strictly **prohibited** in order to preserve the delicate items from light damage. The vivid and detailed historical **scenes** depicted in the oil paintings completely captivated the sophisticated audience.

The seasoned explorer's overarching **view** of the world changed entirely after his last grueling expedition. His initial **departure** was severely delayed because he had to **cancel** his original commercial flight and **undergo** a rigorous medical examination. Although his cautious expedition doctor usually **restricts** him from climbing to high altitudes too fast, he had to meticulously **attach** a heavy oxygen tank to his specialized climbing gear for this particular ascent. When he finally reached his remote **destination**, the isolated indigenous village **introduced** him to a completely different, harmonious way of life. The surrounding snow-capped mountain **scenes** were absolutely **spectacular** to behold. He **derived** a profound sense of inner peace that proved highly **beneficial** to his mental health after months of stress. He urgently wanted to **expand** his anthropological research here, even though local law stated that camping near the sacred ancient ruins was strictly **prohibited**. During a sudden, widespread power **blackout** that night, the brilliant starry sky was the only light source, guiding his thoughts.

The heavy rain is fast **approaching** the small town. We found a safe place to stay, which was a great **relief**. My **primary** goal is to keep everyone dry today. We used this **occasion** to play games inside. I will not **reproach** anyone for forgetting their umbrella.

The **primary** reason for the meeting is to discuss the upcoming festival. As the date is rapidly **approaching**, we have a lot to prepare. You should not **reproach** yourself if things are not perfect yet. We hope the event will be a happy **occasion** for the whole town. Finishing the decorations early brought everyone a huge sense of **relief**.

Finding the lost dog was a huge **relief** for the worried family. Their **primary** concern had been his safety in the cold night. They threw a small party, using the happy **occasion** to thank their neighbors. With winter **approaching**, they bought him a warm coat. They promised never to **reproach** each other for leaving the gate open again.

The manager's harsh **reproach** left the team feeling demoralized before the major presentation. They used the formal corporate **occasion** to clarify their innovative strategy. It was a profound **relief** when the board approved their budget proposal. With the end of the fiscal quarter rapidly **approaching**, time management became crucial. Their **primary** objective now is to ensure seamless implementation across all departments.

She was **previously** a singer before becoming a teacher. To **prolong** the life of your shoes, keep them clean. The bird was very **swift** and flew away fast. It is **premature** to say who will win the game now. We will **ultimately** know the winner tomorrow.

The police made a **swift** decision to close the road. It was **premature** to judge the new student on his first day. He will **ultimately** show us how smart he really is. He **previously** lived in a very small village. Good eating habits can help **prolong** your healthy life.

The company will **ultimately** decide whether to launch the product next month. They want to **prolong** the testing phase to fix any bugs. A **swift** response to customer complaints is crucial for success. The team **previously** struggled with communication issues. Making changes now would be a **premature** move.

Declaring victory at this stage of the negotiations would be highly **premature**. The diplomat, who **previously** served in Europe, understood the complexities. He advised against actions that might unnecessarily **prolong** the tense discussions. The government will **ultimately** have to compromise on several key issues. A **swift** and decisive resolution is necessary to stabilize the global market.

I take a **regular** walk in the park every morning. I enjoy walking in a **leisurely** way without rushing. The bright sun feels like an **eternal** light in the sky. Over a short **span** of time, I finish my coffee. I plan my daily schedule **accordingly** after that.

We took a **leisurely** bike ride by the river on Sunday. Over the **span** of two hours, we saw many beautiful birds. Reading books is a **regular** habit for my brother. When it rains, we change our plans **accordingly**. Some people believe that true love is completely **eternal**.

The poet wrote about the **eternal** beauty of the majestic mountains. The guide warned us about the weather, and we packed our bags **accordingly**. Our **regular** meetings help the project stay strictly on track. In a brief **span** of five years, the city changed completely. Tourists can enjoy a **leisurely** boat tour around the famous lake.

Over the **span** of a single decade, technological advancements reshaped society. The committee holds **regular** sessions to monitor these rapid developments. Policies must be updated **accordingly** to address potential ethical concerns. The debate over privacy vs. security seems to be an **eternal** conflict. Observers often take a **leisurely** approach to analyzing these complex historical trends.

The **primary** objective of the summit was to address the rapidly **approaching** climate crisis. The veteran diplomat, who had **previously** warned about this ecological disaster, used the high-profile **occasion** to demand immediate action. Over the **span** of her career, she had witnessed an **eternal** cycle of political inaction and empty promises. It was **premature** to celebrate the newly signed treaty, as true success would **ultimately** depend on strict enforcement. Some nations sought to **prolong** the negotiation phase, hoping to dilute the environmental regulations. However, the unexpected and **swift** implementation of trade sanctions by allied forces left no room for delay. The delegates realized they could no longer afford their **leisurely** pace of debate. They adjusted their national policies **accordingly** to meet the new, rigorous standards. Achieving a consensus brought a palpable sense of **relief** to the exhausted committee. As a **regular** reminder of their duty, no leader would ever **reproach** themselves for acting too late to save the planet.

I have a **chronic** cough. The doctor said it is not **contagious**. He gave me a pill and told me the right **dosage**. I must not **crush** the pill before taking it. I hope there is no flu **epidemic** this year.

During the flu **epidemic**, many students stayed home. The virus was highly **contagious** among the kids. Please do not **crush** the medicine when you give it to him. Also, make sure the **dosage** is exactly right. This will help cure his **chronic** headache.

The new disease proved to be extremely **contagious** in the crowded city. Doctors prescribed a specific **dosage** of antibiotics for the patients. People with **chronic** illnesses were asked to stay indoors. The sudden **epidemic** caused a lot of public panic. People would rush and sometimes **crush** each other at the pharmacy.

The government attempted to **crush** the spread of misinformation regarding the health crisis. Patients suffering from **chronic** conditions were prioritized for treatment. The rapid onset of the **epidemic** overwhelmed the healthcare system. Because the strain was highly **contagious**, strict quarantine measures were implemented. Physicians carefully monitored the drug **dosage** to prevent adverse reactions.

My **intuition** tells me it will rain today. It is good for your **mental** health to relax. If you are sick, you might have **infected** your friends. A loud dog can **deter** bad people from entering. Some people naturally have high **immunity** to colds.

Good **mental** health is as important as physical health. Locks on doors can **deter** thieves from breaking in. The boy accidentally **infected** his sister with a bad cold. Eating healthy food helps build your body's **immunity**. She used her **intuition** to guess the right answer on the test.

The doctor confirmed that the patient had **infected** several staff members. The vaccine provides long-term **immunity** against the dangerous virus. Sometimes, relying on your **intuition** is better than overthinking. Taking a break is essential for overcoming **mental** exhaustion. High fines are used to **deter** drivers from speeding.

Strict legal penalties are often implemented to **deter** corporate fraud. The sophisticated malware had already **infected** thousands of computer networks worldwide. The high-stress environment took a severe toll on his **mental** well-being. The key witness was granted full **immunity** in exchange for his testimony. The detective relied on his seasoned **intuition** to solve the complex murder case.

I need more **detergent** to wash these dirty clothes. The boy was so scared that he almost **fainted**. It is a natural **instinct** to run from danger. I have to read an **academic** book for my class. Eye color is decided by **heredity**.

Birds fly south in the winter by natural **instinct**. We learned about **heredity** and genes in our science class. You should use a stronger **detergent** to remove that stain. He is busy writing a paper for the new **academic** year. She felt dizzy and suddenly **fainted** in the hot sun.

The professor published his research in a prestigious **academic** journal. The marathon runner was exhausted and **fainted** at the finish line. Her first **instinct** was to protect her younger brother. The study focuses on how **heredity** affects certain physical traits. The company launched a new eco-friendly laundry **detergent**.

The complex role of **heredity** in determining personality traits remains a popular topic. The chemical composition of the industrial **detergent** is strictly regulated. Her brilliant **academic** achievements earned her a full scholarship to the university. After hearing the shocking verdict, the defendant nearly **fainted** in the courtroom. It requires sharp business **instinct** to survive in such a competitive market.

The brilliant researcher, known for her sharp **intuition** in **academic** circles, was investigating a highly **contagious** virus that had sparked a global **epidemic**. People with **chronic** respiratory issues were particularly vulnerable, and the disease took a severe toll on both physical and **mental** health. Her primary goal was to discover a chemical compound, perhaps even something derived from a common industrial **detergent**, that could **crush** the virus's outer shell. She hoped this breakthrough would **deter** the pathogen from spreading further. Early studies on **heredity** suggested that some populations possessed a natural **immunity**, preventing them from getting **infected**. Operating on pure **instinct**, she altered the drug's **dosage** during a critical trial. The pressure in the lab was so immense that her assistant nearly **fainted** from exhaustion. Ultimately, her unconventional approach succeeded, proving that bold innovation is sometimes necessary to save lives.

I use an umbrella as a **shield** from the rain. The doctor **prescribed** some medicine for my cold. I hope to **recover** soon so I can play outside. Drinking warm water will help **restore** my health. Eating fruits builds my body's **resistance**.

It takes time to **recover** from a bad flu. A good sleep can **restore** your energy. Wearing a jacket acts as a **shield** against the cold wind. The doctor **prescribed** antibiotics to fight the infection. Vitamins help increase your **resistance** to diseases.

The specialist **prescribed** a new physical therapy program. The strong armor served as an effective **shield** in battle. Her low **resistance** made her vulnerable to catching a cold. She worked hard to **recover** her lost strength. They aim to **restore** the old building to its original beauty.

Building immunity is crucial to developing **resistance** against bacterial infections. The government implemented policies to **restore** economic stability after the crisis. The athlete struggled to fully **recover** from the devastating knee injury. The psychiatrist **prescribed** a mild sedative to calm the patient's anxiety. A robust legal framework acts as a **shield** against corporate exploitation.

He took a bad **tumble** down the stairs. Luckily, he had no **permanent** injuries. My grandfather is still very **vigorous** and active. Wash your hands to keep the flu **virus** away. People like to **speculate** about what will happen tomorrow.

The dog enjoys a **vigorous** run in the park. Be careful not to take a **tumble** on the wet floor. Fans love to **speculate** about who will win the big game. A new computer **virus** is spreading across the internet. The ink from this pen will leave a **permanent** mark.

Experts **speculate** that the stock market will drop next week. Scientists are studying how the **virus** mutated so quickly. He sought **permanent** employment rather than taking temporary jobs. The coach designed a **vigorous** training routine for the team. The gymnast took an unexpected **tumble** during her floor routine.

The highly contagious **virus** caused a global public health emergency. The tragic accident left him with **permanent** neurological damage. Financial analysts continue to **speculate** on the impact of the impending merger. A sudden market crash caused investors' portfolios to take a steep **tumble**. Only a **vigorous** defense strategy can save the company from bankruptcy.

Water is **essential** for all living things. He bought a **sturdy** wooden table for the kitchen. The old tree had deeply **twisted** branches. Eating too much fast food can cause **obesity**. After a long day of work, I felt terrible **fatigue**.

The hiker wore **sturdy** boots for the rocky mountain trail. The story in the book had a very **twisted** ending. Daily exercise is **essential** for a healthy lifestyle. He drank coffee to fight off his morning **fatigue**. Education plays a big role in preventing childhood **obesity**.

Muscle **fatigue** is common after completing a marathon. They constructed a **sturdy** shelter to protect against the storm. The doctor warned the patient about the severe risks of **obesity**. The politician deliberately **twisted** the facts to win the debate. It is **essential** that we submit the final report by Friday.

The rising rates of adolescent **obesity** pose a major challenge to the healthcare system. Chronic **fatigue** syndrome can severely diminish a person's quality of life. An unbiased jury is absolutely **essential** to the justice system. The ancient fortress, with its **sturdy** stone walls, survived centuries of warfare. The journalist's words were maliciously **twisted** to ruin her professional reputation.

The medical team faced an unprecedented crisis when a lethal **virus** began sweeping through the population. Initial symptoms included severe muscle **fatigue** and an alarming drop in the body's natural **resistance**. Doctors quickly **prescribed** aggressive antiviral treatments to help patients **recover**, though many suffered a sudden **tumble** in their overall health. Experts began to **speculate** whether the pathogen would cause **permanent** damage to the nervous system. To combat the spread, a **vigorous** public health campaign became **essential**. It emphasized the need for a **sturdy** immune system, warning that pre-existing conditions like **obesity** could exacerbate the illness. Researchers worked tirelessly to develop a vaccine that would act as a biological **shield** against the infection. Unfortunately, media outlets occasionally **twisted** the scientific data, causing unnecessary panic. Despite these setbacks, the global community united in a monumental effort to **restore** order and eradicate the deadly disease.

attach.definition: To fasten or join one thing to another.
attach.example: You're required to attach a recent photograph to your application form.
attach.example_tw: 你被要求在申請表上貼上一張最近的照片。

beneficial.definition: Having a helpful or useful effect.
beneficial.example: Cycling is highly beneficial to health and the environment. We should encourage more people to do it.
beneficial.example_tw: 騎自行車對健康和環境非常有益。我們應該鼓勵更多人騎。

cancel.definition: To decide that an organized event will not happen.
cancel.example: I phoned the hotel to cancel my reservation. I've changed my plan.
cancel.example_tw: 我打電話給旅館取消預訂。我改變了計畫。

departure.definition: The act of leaving a place.
departure.example: What is the departure time of the flight to Japan? We'd better make sure of it.
departure.example_tw: 飛往日本的航班出發時間是什麼時候？我們最好確認一下。

undergo.definition: To experience something, especially a change or something unpleasant.
undergo.example: You may have to undergo disappointment and failure before experiencing success.
undergo.example_tw: 在體驗成功之前，你可能必須經歷失望和失敗。

view.definition: An opinion or belief about something.
view.example: His view of life is different from mine. We have different opinions about life.
view.example_tw: 他的人生觀與我不同。我們對生活有不同的看法。

introduced.definition: To bring something into a place for the first time.
introduced.example: The gray squirrel was introduced into Britain from North America. It was not indigenous.
introduced.example_tw: 灰松鼠是從北美引進英國的。牠不是本土物種。

destination.definition: The place where someone or something is going.
destination.example: On account of the traffic jam, they arrived at their destination two hours late.
destination.example_tw: 由於交通阻塞，他們晚了兩個小時到達目的地。

restricts.example: My grandfather restricts himself to three cigarettes a day.
restricts.example_tw: 我祖父限制自己一天只能抽三支菸。

spectacular.example: The spectacular mountain scenery attracts tourists all over the world.
spectacular.example_tw: 壯觀的山景吸引了世界各地的遊客。

prohibited.example: We are prohibited from smoking during working hours. No one can smoke in the office.
prohibited.example_tw: 工作時間我們被禁止吸菸。辦公室裡沒有人可以抽菸。

blackout.definition: A period when there is no electricity supply.
blackout.example: We had a two-hour blackout last night. The electricity supply was cut off then.
blackout.example_tw: 昨晚我們停電了兩個小時。當時電力供應被切斷了。

expand.definition: To increase in size, number, or importance.
expand.example: Traveling enables us to expand our horizons, and so does reading.
expand.example_tw: 旅行使我們能夠拓展視野，閱讀也是如此。

scenes.definition: Views or pictures of a place.
scenes.example: A number of wonderful scenes were strongly imprinted on the poet's mind.
scenes.example_tw: 許多美妙的景色深深印在詩人的腦海中。

derived.definition: To get something from something else.
derived.example: He derived a lot of pleasure from meeting new people. He was really an outgoing person.
derived.example_tw: 他從結識新朋友中獲得了很多樂趣。他真是一個外向的人。

approaching.definition: Coming nearer in distance or time.
approaching.example: The time is fast approaching when we will have to make a decision.
approaching.example_tw: 我們必須做出決定的時間正快速逼近。

occasion.definition: A particular time or opportunity for something to happen.
occasion.example: He seized the occasion to invite her family for dinner.
occasion.example_tw: 他抓住這個機會邀請她的家人共進晚餐。

reproach.definition: To criticize or find fault with someone.
reproach.example: It wasn't your fault. You have nothing to reproach yourself for.
reproach.example_tw: 這不是你的錯。你沒有什麼好自責的。

relief.definition: A feeling of comfort when something painful or stressful ends.
relief.example: This medicine will bring you some relief. You'll feel much better after taking it.
relief.example_tw: 這藥會帶給你一些舒緩。服用後你會覺得好多了。

primary.example: The primary purpose of his visit is to improve trading relations.
primary.example_tw: 他來訪的主要目的是改善貿易關係。

swift.example: They have been swift to deny the rumors. Their announcement was immediate.
swift.example_tw: 他們迅速否認了謠言。他們的聲明是立即的。

premature.example: I think your criticism is a bit premature as we don't know all the details.
premature.example_tw: 我認為你的批評有點過早，因為我們還不知道所有的細節。

previously.definition: At an earlier time or before something else.
previously.example: She was previously employed as a tour guide. But now she's a secretary.
previously.example_tw: 她以前受雇為導遊。但她現在是秘書。

prolong.example: Careful treatment will prolong the life of furniture. You can use the furniture much longer if you treat it well.
prolong.example_tw: 仔細的保養會延長家具的壽命。如果你好好對待它，你可以使用更久。

ultimately.definition: Finally, after a series of things have happened.
ultimately.example: Everything will ultimately depend on what is said at the next meeting. Just wait and see.
ultimately.example_tw: 一切最終將取決於下次會議的內容。等著看吧。

eternal.definition: Lasting forever or for a very long time.
eternal.example: That history repeats itself is a(n) eternal truth.
eternal.example_tw: 歷史會重演是一個永恆的真理。

accordingly.definition: In a way that is suitable or right for the situation.
accordingly.example: He was told to sit tight; accordingly, he didn't dare to move an inch.
accordingly.example_tw: 他被告知要按兵不動；因此，他一動也不敢動。

leisurely.definition: In a relaxed and unhurried way.
leisurely.example: When she strolls leisurely along the beach, she feels herself becoming part of nature.
leisurely.example_tw: 當她悠閒地在海灘上漫步時，她感覺自己成為大自然的一部分。

regular.definition: Happening repeatedly in a fixed pattern.
regular.example: This is not a regular event; it is held only on special occasions.
regular.example_tw: 這不是一個定期的活動；它只在特殊場合舉行。

span.definition: The length of time that something lasts.
span.example: Over a(n) span of three years, the shopping mall was expanded to include several parking lots.
span.example_tw: 在三年的期間內，這家購物中心擴建了幾個停車場。

chronic.definition: (Of an illness) persisting for a long time or constantly recurring.
chronic.example: He was a(n) chronic alcoholic and unable to hold down a job.
chronic.example_tw: 他是一名慢性酗酒者，無法保住一份工作。

contagious.definition: Spread from one person or organism to another by direct or indirect contact.
contagious.example: Be careful! The patient is still highly contagious. Don't get too close to him.
contagious.example_tw: 小心！這位病人仍然具有高度傳染性。不要太靠近他。

dosage.definition: The size or frequency of a dose of a medicine or drug.
dosage.example: Remember to take the correct dosage or it will not work.
dosage.example_tw: 記得服用正確的劑量，否則它將不會有效。

crush.definition: To press or squeeze something so hard that it is damaged or injured.
crush.example: Don't crush the box. There are eggs inside.
crush.example_tw: 不要壓壞這個盒子。裡面有雞蛋。

epidemic.definition: A widespread occurrence of an infectious disease in a community at a particular time.
epidemic.example: Hundreds of people died during last year's flu epidemic.
epidemic.example_tw: 去年流感疫情期間有數百人死亡。

deter.definition: To discourage someone from doing something, typically by instilling doubt or fear of the consequences.
deter.example: We need severe punishments to deter people from dealing drugs.
deter.example_tw: 我們需要嚴厲的懲罰來嚇阻人們販毒。

immunity.definition: Protection or exemption from something, especially an obligation or penalty.
immunity.example: The young man was granted immunity from prosecution. He wouldn't be charged with any crime.
immunity.example_tw: 這名年輕人獲得了免於起訴的豁免權。他將不會被控告任何罪名。

infected.definition: To pass a disease to a person, animal, or plant.
infected.example: She had a bad cold last week and probably infected me.
infected.example_tw: 她上週得了重感冒，可能傳染給我了。

intuition.definition: The ability to understand something immediately, without the need for conscious reasoning.
intuition.example: She knew, by intuition, about his illness although he never mentioned it.
intuition.example_tw: 儘管他從未提及，她憑直覺就知道他的病情。

mental.example: Many people suffer from some form of mental illness during their lives.
mental.example_tw: 許多人在一生中都會患有某種形式的精神疾病。

academic.example: In Britain, the academic year runs from October to July. Students go to school during that period.
academic.example_tw: 在英國，學年從十月到七月。學生在那段期間上學。

heredity.definition: The passing on of physical or mental characteristics genetically from one generation to another.
heredity.example: Heredity shows us that living things have the ability to pass on their own qualities from parent to child in the cells of the body.
heredity.example_tw: 遺傳學向我們展示了生物有能力將自身的特徵透過身體細胞從父母傳給孩子。

detergent.example: Jenny always uses the detergent to clean the stains on the clothing. She thinks that it works well.
detergent.example_tw: 珍妮總是使用清潔劑來清除衣服上的汙漬。她覺得效果很好。

instinct.definition: An innate, typically fixed pattern of behavior in animals in response to certain stimuli.
instinct.example: Don't ask me; follow your instinct and do what you think is right.
instinct.example_tw: 不要問我；跟隨你的本能，做你認為對的事。

fainted.definition: Lost consciousness for a short time because of a temporarily insufficient supply of oxygen to the brain.
fainted.example: On hearing the tragic news, he fainted and didn't come to himself for ten minutes.
fainted.example_tw: 聽到這個悲慘的消息，他昏倒了，十分鐘後才甦醒過來。

shield.definition: A broad piece of defensive armor carried on the arm; a person or thing providing protection.
shield.example: Dark glasses are an effective shield against the glare of the sun.
shield.example_tw: 墨鏡是抵擋刺眼陽光的有效防護。

prescribed.definition: (Of a medical practitioner) advise and authorize the use of (a medicine or treatment) for someone.
prescribed.example: The doctor prescribed some pills for my allergy. I felt better after taking them.
prescribed.example_tw: 醫生開了一些藥丸來治療我的過敏。我吃完後感覺好多了。

recover.definition: To return to a normal state of health, mind, or strength.
recover.example: You need daily exercise if you're going to recover the use of your legs.
recover.example_tw: 如果你想恢復雙腿的功能，你需要每天運動。

resistance.definition: The ability not to be affected by something, especially adversely.
resistance.example: My resistance to colds has been low. It's very easy for me to catch a cold.
resistance.example_tw: 我對感冒的抵抗力一直很低。我很容易感冒。

restore.definition: To bring back or return (someone or something) to a former condition, place, or position.
restore.example: This proposal would restore the tax advantages that small business used to enjoy.
restore.example_tw: 這項提議將恢復小型企業過去享有的稅收優勢。

speculate.definition: To form a theory or conjecture about a subject without firm evidence.
speculate.example: We don't know all the circumstances, so it would be pointless to speculate on what happened.
speculate.example_tw: 我們不知道所有的情況，所以猜測發生了什麼事是沒有意義的。

vigorous.definition: Strong, healthy, and full of energy.
vigorous.example: For a man of seventy, he is still surprisingly vigorous. He is strong enough to run a marathon.
vigorous.example_tw: 對於一個七十歲的人來說，他依然出奇地精力充沛。他強壯到可以跑馬拉松。

permanent.definition: Lasting or intended to last or remain unchanged indefinitely.
permanent.example: The accident has caused permanent damage to Mr. Smith's legs. He can never walk again.
permanent.example_tw: 這場意外對史密斯先生的雙腿造成了永久性的傷害。他再也無法走路了。

tumble.example: I had a nasty tumble and hurt my knee.
tumble.example_tw: 我重重地摔了一跤，傷了膝蓋。

virus.definition: An infective agent that typically consists of a nucleic acid molecule in a protein coat and is able to multiply only within the living cells of a host.
virus.example: The AIDS virus spread quickly among human beings.
virus.example_tw: 愛滋病毒在人類之間迅速傳播。

obesity.definition: The condition of being grossly fat or overweight.
obesity.example: Constant exercise and proper diet are of great help in preventing obesity.
obesity.example_tw: 持續的運動和適當的飲食對預防肥胖有很大的幫助。

essential.example: There's no denying that these nutrients are essential to our health. In other words, they are indispensable.
essential.example_tw: 無可否認，這些營養素對我們的健康是不可或缺的。換句話說，它們是必需的。

sturdy.definition: (Of a person or their body) strongly and solidly built.
sturdy.example: The sturdy young man removed the rock without effort.
sturdy.example_tw: 那個結實的年輕人不費吹灰之力就移開了岩石。

fatigue.definition: Extreme tiredness, typically resulting from mental or physical exertion or illness.
fatigue.example: If you want to recover from the fatigue of the journey, you will have to take a good rest.
fatigue.example_tw: 如果你想從旅途的疲倦中恢復過來，你必須好好休息。

twisted.definition: Deliberately changed the meaning of (words) in order to give a false impression.
twisted.example: The newspapers deliberately twisted her words to make her look guilty.
twisted.example_tw: 報紙故意扭曲她的話，讓她看起來有罪。
